key_insight: A PCYS interpolation-table surrogate can replace expensive VPSC micro-scale calculations in FEM while still tracking irradiation-driven anisotropic deformation with high fidelity.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, engineering-scale prediction of zirconium alloy component swelling and creep in nuclear reactor internals, supporting safer design and lifespan assessment.
method: Implemented a 5D interpolation-table surrogate in Code_Aster to emulate instantaneous viscoplastic response.
Used periodic recalibration plus first-order Taylor linearization to follow microstructural drift from irradiation.
Benchmarked the surrogate against VPSC-FEM under continuous high-dose irradiation and spacer-grid structural simulations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports relative errors below 1% for representative deformation paths and meaningful computational savings in a spacer-grid domain study.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on the validity of the interpolation domain and recalibration scheme, so extrapolation beyond trained irradiation/deformation states may be unreliable.
paper_title: Accelerating Multi-scale Simulations of Nuclear Components via PCYS Interpolation Tables
